label: baseline
notes: greedy toxicity low across all slices, but sampled (K=16) reveals much higher latent toxicity especially on rtp_challenging (support_rate=0.84, mean_max=0.775) -- plausible detox target, real gap between greedy and sampled behavior

label: sft
notes: vanilla SFT round A (baseline documentation): greedy toxicity collapsed near-zero across all slices but so did diversity -- 40% unique (30/75), single completion 'I'm sorry, I don't understand.' covers 45% (34/75) of all outputs. Tracked 35-prompt eyeball: 34/35 collapsed to a hedge template regardless of prompt content/toxicity bucket, only 1 genuine answer. Classic warning-sign pattern (toxicity near-zero AND uniqueness collapsing together) -- this is the documented vanilla-data baseline failure mode, not the foundation DPO/RM/PPO will build on.

label: sft_diverse
notes: Round B on sft_diverse.jsonl: exact-string uniqueness solved (75/75, 35/35 vs Round A's 40%/1 dominant string) but semantic collapse persists -- 32/35 tracked prompts still open with a refusal template regardless of content, incl. benign ones, vs 34/35 in Round A

label: dpo
notes: DPO on dpo_diverse.jsonl from sft_diverse: lower toxicity on every scalar metric (rtp support_rate 0.44->0.00) but entrenches SFT's refusal collapse -- refusal-template rate flat (33/35->34/35) and rhetorical-structure convergence nearly doubles (8/35->15/35 use 'harmful and inappropriate' framing almost verbatim), reproducing STAGEWISE_ANALYSIS.md sec11's entrenchment finding

label: rm_harmlessness
notes: single-axis (chosen vs rejected_toxic), same contrast GUIDE.md Stage 4 literally asks for -- fails its own red-team gate: dpo_political_deflection attractor scores at the 93rd percentile vs genuine responses, 2 more attractors at 77th/87th. Not safe for PPO alone.

label: rm_helpfulness
notes: dual-RM fix (chosen vs rejected_evasive, attempt_2 Phase 4 ported onto attempt_3's own data+attractors): passes red-team gate cleanly (all 4 attractors well below genuine-response percentile), but has its own blind spot -- prefers toxic-but-specific completions on 5/100 real pairs (vs attempt_2's 62.5%, likely bc diversified chosen is already more substantive). Neither RM safe alone; Stage 5 needs the Lagrangian dual-RM combiner.

label: dpo_v2
notes: three-way DPO (dpo_v2), vs own Stage 3 DPO: uniqueness 75/75=100% vs 74/75=98.7% (marginal improvement); refusal-template rate 34/35 identical to Stage 3 -- templating problem essentially unmoved, consistent with LOGBOOK's Stage 2 prediction that data-side fixes barely touch this

label: ppo_detoxify
notes: inv:detoxify, 100 steps: moderate collapse onto system-prompt echo ('You are a helpful assistant...I am a member of the community/language model AI') -- 68/75 unique, top string x3

label: ppo_rm
notes: rm:rm_harmlessness (single-axis, unbounded), 100 steps: near-total collapse -- 3/75 unique, one verbatim string ('I am sorry, but I am unable to provide personalized support...') covers 70/75=93.3% of all completions regardless of prompt. Entropy 2.95->0.02-0.03, val reward climbed to +32.7 (unbounded). Exactly the collapse LOGBOOK's Stage 4 decision-gate predicted.

label: ppo_custom
notes: custom:tasks.task8_custom_reward (relevance gate + repetition/length-cap penalties), 100 steps: HIGHEST uniqueness (72/75) but WORST sampled toxicity of all three variants (rtp_challenging support_rate 0.68 vs 0.04/0.00) -- collapsed into garbled, incoherent system-prompt-echo loops with foreign-script/control-token fragments ('.CompareTag'). High uniqueness here is an artifact of incoherence, not health -- defenses didn't prevent collapse, just changed its shape.

label: ppo_fixed
notes: dual_lagrangian (help+harm RM, Lagrangian combiner + diversity penalty + KL-anchor/entropy-bonus fix), 100 steps: closes Stage 5's low-entropy narrow-template collapse (entropy climbed 2.97->6.10 instead of collapsing to ~0) but opens a NEW, different failure mode -- high-entropy collapse into incoherent, often non-English (Russian-script) text. 74/75 exact-string unique (looks great) but that's the same trap as ppo_custom: uniqueness measures string diversity, not coherence/language/on-topicness. 55/55 fresh out-of-distribution prompts (none resembling training data) ALSO came back 100% Russian-script, mean Detoxify 0.0053 (reads as 'safe' but isn't actually functioning as an assistant). Lagrangian lambda stayed at 0.0 the whole run (cost_ema never exceeded target, so the harmlessness constraint was never actually binding -- the fix's core mechanism was inert for this run, similar in spirit to the original KL-wiring bug this project's own verl_runner_v2 was built to fix).
